es.example: De ahora en adelante llegaré puntual.
es.exampleTranslation: From now on I'll arrive on time.

it.exampleTranslation: From now on I'll be on time.

bridge.note: Both draw a line at now and point past it — Italian toward 'then' (poi), Spanish toward 'forward' (adelante). Same doorway, facing different words.

interference.es_to_it: The frame matches (de/d' … en/in …) but the last word doesn't translate: Italian ends in poi (then), Spanish in adelante (forward). 'De ahora en después' is nobody's Spanish.
interference.it_to_es: 'D'ora in avanti' actually exists in Italian too — but calquing the full Spanish gives 'd'adesso in avanti', which no one says.

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm. Cue drafted by Claude 2026-07-20 from the entry's meaning and bridge — English situational framing, not a language claim, so it needs editorial taste rather than native review. Override freely.

cue: How do you announce a change starting now?
